fault_hypothesis: The condition on line 62 uses `>` instead of `>=`, so when `len(recent) == limit` the function incorrectly allows the task instead of denying it.

constraints.intent: When len(recent) equals limit the branch should be taken (denied); the > operator misses the equality case.
After the if-branch is skipped (else path), the number of recent events must be strictly less than the limit, not merely not greater.
Confirm recent and limit are valid before the comparison so the condition bug is isolated to the operator choice.
The task must be denied whenever recent events are greater than OR equal to the limit.
